title: Sustainable Aviation Buyers Alliance Launches Platform to Help COP30 Attendees Cut Flight Emissions
content: Sustainable Aviation Buyers Alliance (SABA) announced a new initiative to enable COP30 attendees to support the clean energy transition and address emissions from their air travel to and from the UN-hosted climate convening in BelÃ©m, Brazil in November 2025. The COP30 SAF platform empowers conference attendees to calculate and address their aviation-related emissions by supporting sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) that meets SABAâ€™s world leading sustainability criteria. In this first of a kind opportunity, the platform is available to all COP30 attendees, including those who are not currently SABA members, as part of a concentrated effort to raise the visibility of SAF and scale funding and adoption of this key climate technology. SABAâ€™s COP30 initiative is unique because it gives conference goers all the resources they need to decarbonize their COP air travel on a single platform. Attendees first estimate their flight emissions using one of two methods. For commercial flights, travelers will use Googleâ€™s Travel Impact Model, an industry-leading calculation tool, while travelers on private jets will use the Eurocontrol Small Emitters tool. The platform supports those traveling via commercial and private aircraft â€“ crucial flexibility given that as many as 65 private jets travel to COP in a given year.
author: Analysts Account
